amendments.normalized_body: `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:347-378` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2280-2318` serialize completion details with a raw `", "`-delimited string, but the only protection against `project_id`/`run_id`/`plan_hash` containing that delimiter is `debug_assert!`. In release builds those checks disappear, and `ProjectId::new` / `RunId::new` still accept commas and spaces (`src/shared/domain.rs:804-838`). A legal id like `"foo, bar"` makes `CompletionJournalDetails::parse` fail, which means the repair path in `append_event_if_missing` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2416-2493`) cannot merge or backfill replayed completion events. The result is duplicate completion journal rows instead of the intended idempotent repair whenever a replay adds missing `run_id`, `plan_hash`, or `outcome_detail`.

`FsTaskRunLineageStore::append_task_run` still writes whatever `TaskRunEntry` it is handed (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2682-2691`). Because `milestone_id` remains optional and `read_task_runs_from_path` only backfills it in memory after deserialization (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2525-2533`), any direct caller of the public append API can still create brand-new NDJSON rows with no explicit `milestone_id`. That violates the new self-contained-entry invariant introduced by this task and leaves on-disk lineage incomplete until some later rewrite happens to touch the row.

final_accepted_amendments.normalized_body: `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:347-378` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2280-2318` serialize completion details with a raw `", "`-delimited string, but the only protection against `project_id`/`run_id`/`plan_hash` containing that delimiter is `debug_assert!`. In release builds those checks disappear, and `ProjectId::new` / `RunId::new` still accept commas and spaces (`src/shared/domain.rs:804-838`). A legal id like `"foo, bar"` makes `CompletionJournalDetails::parse` fail, which means the repair path in `append_event_if_missing` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2416-2493`) cannot merge or backfill replayed completion events. The result is duplicate completion journal rows instead of the intended idempotent repair whenever a replay adds missing `run_id`, `plan_hash`, or `outcome_detail`.

`FsTaskRunLineageStore::append_task_run` still writes whatever `TaskRunEntry` it is handed (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2682-2691`). Because `milestone_id` remains optional and `read_task_runs_from_path` only backfills it in memory after deserialization (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2525-2533`), any direct caller of the public append API can still create brand-new NDJSON rows with no explicit `milestone_id`. That violates the new self-contained-entry invariant introduced by this task and leaves on-disk lineage incomplete until some later rewrite happens to touch the row.

payload.summary: Final review accepted 2 amendment(s); restart required.
